1990_09_02-India_Mumbai_Birla_House-Surrendering_Everything_Mumbai-st2-vr01

Radhanath Swami Transcriptions Template

Meta Data v9

Event Name/Title

Surrender Everything

Event Date

02 Sep 1990

Event Location

India, Mumbai, Birla House

Event Type

Private

Transcriber

Bhuvaneshwari Devi Dasi

Completed

01 Dec 2021

Source Audio

90-078-Surrendering Everything (Mumbai)-OT-dt_90_09_02.mp3

Source Video

NA

Verse

Dasa avatar Stotram, Bg 5.29,Bg 4.34

Tags – Places

Sutala, Heaven, Garbhodhak ocean

Tags – Pastimes

Mohini murti incarnation of Lord, Appearance of Lord Vamana dev, Churning of ocean of milk

Tags – Persons

Aditi, Kashyap Muni, Vamana dev, Shukracharya, Bali Maharaja, Vindyabali, Asuras, Demigods, Indra, Lord Narayana, Prahlad Maharaja

Tags – Themes

Surrender, Faith, Happiness is within, material desires are like fire, Krishna is the controller, Proprietor and enjoyer, world is a place of misery, hankering and lamenting nature of mind, nature of name and fame in material world, Laxmi devi is restless, Atmanivedan -highest form of surrender

Lecture Transcription

Glorification of Lord Vamanadev by Srila Jayadeva Goswami

kesava dhrita-vamana-rupa jaya jagadisa hare. [Song name: Sri Dasavatara-stotra from Gita-govinda by Jayadeva Gosvami] Today we are singing a beautiful prayer which is offered in great devotion by that great vaisnava acharya Srila Jayadeva Goswami. Jayadeva Goswami is very famous for his beautiful glorification of the Vrindavan Lila of Sri Sri Radha Govindaji entitled “Gita-Govinda." In the introduction to the “Gita-Govinda”, Srila Jayadeva Goswami offers his prayers to the ten principal incarnations of the Lord. It is called the “Sri Dasavatar Stotram."

Churning of the Ocean of Milk

Today is the divine appearance day of one of these great incarnations of Godhead, Sri Vamanadev. The story is in great detail found in the Srimad Bhagavatam. It is a very long story. But today I would like to, in summary, explain some of the highlights of this [these] wonderful narrations. We know, how at one time, because the demons became very powerful, the Demigods approach Lord Narayana, explaining how the Asuras had taken away all of their possessions. At that time the Supreme Lord Narayana instructed the Demigods to make truce with the demons under the pretence that together they would churn the ocean of milk; through the process of churning the ocean of milk nectar would be created and he assured the demigods that they would gain that nectar. Anyone who drinks this 'Amrit' would become very powerful and unconquerable. So this is a long story of how the ocean of milk was churned and alas when the nectar came out by the divine hands of Dhanvantari, that great avatar of Vishnu. The demons who were more powerful than the demigods at that time they grasped the pot of Nectar from Dhanvantari. Of course, although demons can often times make policies to unite with one another ultimately they are all envious of one another and they are fearful of one another. So the demons could not decide who would drink the nectar first. So, they were all fighting amongst themselves. At that time the demigods found themselves in a hopeless situation. So again they appealed to Lord Narayana, “What are we to do? Now the demons the asuras are already more powerful than us, if they drink this nectar will be endlessly hopeless.” So Lord Narayana assumed the beautiful form of Mohini murti.

Lord assumes the form of Mohini murti and cheats Asuras

In this form she was so beautiful that the demons were completely captivated by her attractive features. They could not control their mind and their senses. So ultimately with the desire to please her because she was glancing each demon in such a way that indicated, “I am only attracted to you”, they all agreed, “Let us give the nectar to you and whoever you decide, you should give it to that person.” Now every demon was thinking, 'She will naturally give it to me because she is looking at me with such love in her eyes.' Mohini murti took the nectar and gave it to all the demigods! The demons became very much disturbed by this and then Mohini murti assumed her original form of Narayana and went away. Then the demons realized they had been cheated by God.

Demons fight with Demigods and lose the war

So then, there was a great war. But, because the demigods had just drunk that nectar they defeated the demons very easily and they killed almost all of them. Even the leader of the demons who was at that time was the great Bali Maharaja, He was even defeated. But Shukracharya who was the master of the demons, what you may call a spiritual master. There are different types of spiritual masters. But he performed the great sacrifice in which he brought all of the demons back to life. He repaired their limbs and brought them back to life. And then he instructed Bali Maharaja that, “If you want to become very powerful, you have to perform a great sacrifice to please the Brahmans. If the Brahmans are pleased, you attain what is called Brahma Tejas, the power of Brahmans then you will become unconquerable."

Demon king, Bali defeats demigods after gaining power from Sacrifice

So Bali Maharaja performed this great great sacrifice and in the performance of the sacrifice, he became invincible. His limbs became like steel. He had such power and such potency that he assembled a vast vast army of millions and millions of countless Asuras and they all marched upon the abode of Indra, the king of heaven. When Indra looked out of his from his palace and he saw these multitudes of demons headed by Bali Maharaja, who was effulgent like millions of suns, Indra became very frightened and he turned to Brihaspati and he said to Brihaspati, “How I am possibly going to counteract this great attack by Bali Maharaja? Look at his forces his own body alone looks like it can conquer all the fourteen worlds." Brihaspati said that, “Bali Maharaj has pleased the descendants of Bhrigu. He has satisfied his guru, Shukracharya and the Brahmans. Therefore you have no power to defend yourselves against him.” You see, even the greatest asuras they know that their power comes by giving pleasure to higher powers. We find this throughout history. Hiranyakashipu by pleasing Lord Brahma, he attained great power. Ravana by pleasing Brahma and Shankar, he attained great power. And of course the greatest power is the power from the sages and the Brahmans. So Brihaspati knowing well this great science, he told Indra, “The best thing that you can do is run away and hide. It is only when the Supreme Personality of Godhead favours you that you can ever gain your property back. Until then just be patient.”

Grief of Aditi, the mother of Demigods

So Indra and all the demigods went into hiding and Bali Maharaja simply marched into the city of Indra and marched onto all the planets of the Devatas and became the king of the universe. Now you know the mother of Bali Maharaja is Diti and the mother of Indra is Aditi. They are both sisters, not sisters ya they are both the wives of Kashyap muni. So, Kashyap Muni after doing his tapasya came back to the hermitage one day and he saw his beautiful wife Aditi looking fair white, she was lean and thin. She looked like she was in sad state of lamentation. And Kashyap Muni being a very protective and honest husband, he was feeling very great compassion and sympathy for her. So He began to enquire, “What is it that has caused you such distress?” And She explained, “My children, the demigods, I cannot see them anymore. They are all hiding somewhere and they are in a homeless and in poverty-stricken state. How can I be satisfied in my life when my children are in such a condition? So she requested Kashyap muni to find some answer by which the demigods could again regain their abodes."

Performance of Payo-vrata sacrifice by Aditi to please Vishnu

So Kashyap muni began to contemplate and he explained to Aditi, his wife that "It is only by the mercy of the Lord that the great things are done in this world. With the mercy of God, there is no power that can conquer us." From the material point of view, Brahmatejas is most powerful force there is. But even when everything materially is against us we should never feel hopeless. Because by the grace of God nothing is impossible. Vishnu Shakti, the power of the Lord is supreme. So Kashyap told his wife that,” You perform the Payo-vrata sacrifice. By performing this Payo-vrata you will gain the mercy of Lord Narayana and then all of your desires will be fulfilled.” This was the sacrifice that would last for twelve days and she fasted only on milk and worship Lord Narayana, by chanting his holy names and by worshiping his beautiful form.

Lord Vishnu appears as son of Kashyap and Aditi as Lord Vamana

At the end of that twelve days, the Supreme Lord of Vaikuntha, Sri Vishnu appeared to her in his most splendid form of four-arms decorated with the conchshell, the disc, the club and the lotus flower. He had a beautiful blackish complexion, lotus eyes and wonderful golden crown and the Kaustubhamani jewel hanging from his most munificent chest. Upon seeing the beautiful form of the Lord, Aditi worshiped it with great devotion. Then the Lord knowing her desire promised that he would personally take birth within her womb and by his own divine mercy fulfill all of her desires. So at that time the Supreme Lord entered into the body of Kashyap muni and Kashyap, who is in a state of samadhi, could feel the presence of God within himself. Then he united with Aditi and through the process he transferred the Lord’s presence into her womb and she became most effulgent and most beautiful because she became the very abode of God. After a short time from the womb the Lord appeared outside of her womb again in his beautiful form as four-armed Narayana. There he explained that he had come to fulfill all of their desires. Kashyap and Aditi offered their prostrated obeisances and then the Lord right before their eyes transformed his form into that of a very beautiful golden colored dwarf. This auspicious Lila took placed on the Shravana Dwadasi which is the day we are celebrating today.

Lord Vamanadev visits Bali, the king of Demons seeking charity

When Lord Vamanadev appeared, all the Devatas and all the great saints and sages they came to that place and they began to offer beautiful gifts and praises to the Lord. And then Lord Vamanadev in his form of a beautiful Brahman dwarf began the natural occupation of a Brahman, which is to beg alms. So he went to the place of Bali Maharaja, who was then engaged in another sacrifice. He came to the door of Bali Maharaja with his begging bowl and Bali answered, “Ah! You are a brahmana. I am always charitable to the brahmanas, how may I serve you? Ask anything you like. I am the controller of the universe. I’ll fulfill all of your desires.” Vamanadev said, “My dear Bali, I require only three steps of land from you." Bali Maharaja was somehow insulted by this request. After all he performed such tremendous tapasya and sacrifices just for the prestige of conquering the entire universe and when someone comes to such a prestigious person and ask for something very small, it is like an insult. Bali Maharaja said, “Why you are asking for three steps of land? And you are a dwarf, dwarf means very small. And your steps are not even those of a child. If you like I can give you a beautiful palace, most wonderful family. If you want I can give you riches of the king of heaven, Indra. I can give you your own planet. I am the controller of all the material facilities within the entire universe. Anything you want is yours, just ask me. Why are you asking for such a crazy, small request of three steps?”

Vamanadeva's instructs Bali on Source of Happiness

At that time Vamanadev spoke. He said, “Bali maharaja, you are a great fool because you do not understand that happiness comes from within and if you are not finding happiness within, you will not be happy under any condition of life." He said, “I have found peace within myself. Therefore, whether I have nothing or whether I have three steps, whether I control the whole universe, I am satisfied. But foolish people like you who have not found the real wealth of happiness within themselves, there is no arrangement within this entire creation that can make them satisfied. You have conquered the entire universe and still you want more, still you are dissatisfied." Krishna says in Gita that lust for material desire is like fire. It is insatiable, the more you feed it, the hungrier it becomes. Vamanadev is instructing to all of us, the utter futility of trying to find happiness in the sense objects through any position in this world and yet people are so crazy and foolish that they spend their entire lives under the illusion of Maya - simply trying to find some happiness by bigger name and fame, by more money and by more sense enjoyment. But ultimately as Govinda Das has prayed in his beautiful song, e dhana, yaubana, putra, parijana ithe ki āche paratīti re kamala-dala-jala, jīvana ṭalamala bhajahū hari-pada nīti re [Song Name: Bhaja Hu Re Mana by Govind Das Kaviraja stanza 3] “My whole life I have served my family, my society striving for more and more wealth and more and more prestige in this world and now at the end of my life all I have to show for it is frustration and anxiety." This world is duhkhalayam asasvatam [Bg 8.15] it is a place of misery because everything is temporary and the greatest insanity is that people are trying to find happiness in a place of misery. The more you become attached to something, the more you must suffer when it is taken away and it will be taken away. Even when you have it, you are never satisfied, you always desiring something else. Hankering and lamenting - this is the business of the mind of this material world. Hankering for what you want and then lamenting for what you lose. And in the interim period when you have it, you are usually fearful because you know someday it's going to be lost.

Material Wealth, Name and Fame are temporary and give misery


We see even the greatest persons; just like in America athletics are very popular in America. So, there was one man who was recently, Baseball is the most popular sport in America for the last hundred years. There was one man who broke the most difficult record to break in all of baseball. The record was originally made in 1932 or something and no one had been able to come close to it. This man broke the record. He became the national hero of America. He became so famous. He was on the front page of every magazine, every newspaper, every talk show in America, on Television, and on Radio. He was the number one most famous man in all of the United States. And then about six months later they found out that he had well during his career in baseball he had been gambling and according to baseball, if you gamble it's like the worst offense because you are betting on teams and you are playing that means may be you are losing on purpose and all these other things. So, what happened when they found out he was gambling. It became such big news that he was on the front page of every newspaper and every magazine. It was the biggest news in America that this man is a cheater and rascal as he has been gambling. He was brought to a court and he was put in a jail. Now his very name makes people think, “Ah, just see what he has done!" First all the children made him hero and then he has completely perverted the whole sports-man image. So he became the most unpopular within six months of being the most popular. Ronald Regan, he was the most popular president in the last so many decades then all of a sudden this Iran [indistinct 23.18] came up and he became the most unpopular president. So, this is the nature of fame and name in this material world. Caṣcalam - Lakshmi Devi is the proprietor of wealth, name and fame. Caṣcalam means she is very flickering and very unsteady. One day, she is with you, one day she is with somebody else. You cannot hold on to these things. No matter how hard you try beyond your power, it is the nature of the world that trying to hold onto your position, or your wealth, or your fame is like trying to hold onto sands in a desert. No matter how hard you squeeze, the faster it slips through your fingers. But it will slip through your fingers.

Vamanadeva’s instruction to Bali on Principle of Self-satisfaction

Vamanadev is explaining to Bali maharaja that, “You think, you are so great that you conquered Indra, you conquered the entire universe, you are the wealthiest, the most powerful and the most famous man anywhere around. You think that "I am coming as a beggar because I need something from you. As far as I am concerned you are nothing. If you do not have self realization and peace of mind and love of God, you are in poverty. So just give me three steps of land, I’ll be satisfied." So Bali Maharaja said, “Yes, yes then you take your three steps your tiny little steps."

Shukracharya, guru of Bali instruct Bali not to give charity

At that time Shukracharya, who is the spiritual master of Bali maharaja he could figure out what was happening - that this is Vishnu and he is coming to cheat us. So, he told Bali, “Do not give Him anything. He is Narayana. He will cheat you. He will take everything away. I guarantee, I am your guru. It’s my order, do not give him." Bali Maharaja began to think, “What?" He began to think, contemplate, first of all this is a Brahmachari. And Bali Maharaja was thinking if the Grihastas do not properly respect and serve the Brahmacharis they will lose everything. They have no dignity, no integrity. Even with all their wealth and power, if they lose their dignity and their integrity, it’s all useless. Even from the material point of view and he has come to me. And he is a Brahman. He is a Brahmachari and if he really is Vishnu then everything already belongs to him anyway. Who am I to say no?”Shukracharya, he was motivated. Because you know, some so called gurus, they are attracted to the wealth of their disciples. So, Shukracharya was thinking that, 'If you are poor I become poor also. So therefore you must obey me, do not give him anything. No obligation to give him." And Shukracharya began to recite so many rules and regulations of the Vedas to justify Bali Maharaja to take back his promise from Vamanadev. And then Bali Maharaja he said, “Shukracharya, the Guru is supposed to tell you to surrender to Vishnu, and you are telling me not to surrender to Vishnu. Therefore you are a bogus guru. I reject you." Then Shukracharya became very angry and said, “I curse you that you will lose everything." And then Bali Maharaja just let him go.

Bali surrenders at the Lotus Feet of Lord Vamanadev

Then Bali Maharaja turned to Vamana and said, “Now, you take your three steps." At that time Lord Vamanadev began to expand larger and larger and larger in size, until his body was so big that nobody could see the beginning and the end of it. And with his first step he covered all the lower and middle planetary systems. With his second step he covered the entire upper planetary system and he reached the very end of the universe. At that time his foot touched the very covering of the universe, which is the border. The universes are all flowing in the Garbhdoka Ocean, the casual ocean which is the perspiration of Karanodakshayi, it is actually the Karanodak Ocean, and it is the perspiration of Vishnu himself. And when he pierced the covering of the universe the casual ocean began to leak into the universe. That was the holy river Ganges and the first thing she did after it leaked is it fell on the feet of Vamanadev and washed his lotus feet. Therefore the Ganges is considered not only to be the directly the perspiration of the Vishnu but it is the water that had directly washed the feet of the Supreme Lord Vamanadev. Therefore every drop of Ganges water is Charnamrita - holy foot wash of the Lord. So, then Vamanadev came back and reduced to his size of a dwarf and he looked at Bali Maharaja and said, “You promised me three steps of land, with my two steps I covered everything you owned. Therefore you owe me one step, where should I put it. You are a thief, you deserve to be punished." Because at this time, Vamanadev he was now the Lord of the universe, he took Bali’s position and Garuda understanding the mind of Vamanadev, took the snake of Varuna which became like a rope and he tied it around Bali Maharaja and arrested him and brought him to court. There it was explained, “You are thief, you are a criminal, you are dishonest, you are a liar. You promised me three steps, where will the other step go?" Bali Maharaja was very humble. He began to offer his prayers to the Lord. At that time, Prahlad Maharaja, who was the great grandfather of Bali, actually the grandfather of Bali? Prahalad Maharaj’s son was Virochana and Virochana’s son was Bali. So, Prahalad, his grandfather began to explain to Bali Maharaja, “You are very very lucky that all these material possessions are all like such a burden upon you. But Vishnu has liberated you from all of them. Now you have the chance to surrender totally at his lotus feet. Therefore I consider you most fortunate." Then his own wife Vindyabali she also began to praise the good fortune of Bali Maharaja.

Vamanadev reciprocates Bali’s surrender by being his doorkeeper

Vamanadev explained to Bali that, “I have taken everything away from you but I’ll give you one planet it is the lower planets but it is higher than the heavenly planets, it is called Sutala. But still you have not told me where to put my third step." At that time Bali Maharaja he exhibited the highest principle of surrender. There are nine processes of devotional service - Sravanam, Kirtanam, Smarnam, Vandanaṁ, Padsevanam, Dasyam, Pujanam, Sakhyam, and Atmanivedan. The culmination of all the nine processes of devotional service is Atmanivedan, which means to surrender everything to the Lord and Bali maharaja is considered to be the topmost example of this devotion. He said, “My Lord. Please take your lotus foot and place it on top of my head. I surrender to you." At that time the Lord bestowed Bali Maharaja with the greatest benediction by placing his lotus foot upon his head and Bali Maharaja thus attained the supreme perfection of life.

True wisdom means to know everything belongs to Krishna

Whatever you have, it is Krishna’s. Surrender means to simply accept the truth. Do not surrender means to live a lie. The truth is that Krishna is the proprietor of everything. sarva-loka-maheśvaram [Bg 5.28] He is the creator, preserver and annihilator of everything that exists. To think that anything is ours, whether be our body, our intelligence, our strength, our wealth, our reputation, is the greatest illusion. But to accept that everything is meant for the pleasure of God, everything is meant for his service that is the truth. So Bali maharaja accepted the Supreme truth and he surrendered everything to the Lord. Of course, from the material point of view it appears that it is a great risk when you surrender to the Lord. But one must have faith. The basis of bhakti is faith. Without faith there is no meaning to our spirituality. Sometimes people are attracted to mysticism. But actually as long as we are depending on those things we are never strongly founded in faith in God. Faith in God means faith in his words. Therefore in surrendering to the Lord there is always materially a great risk. But if one has faith that there is no risk at all, one must be willing to make this leap of faith in order to make advancement in spiritual life. Bali Maharaja risked losing everything but he had faith that God is good. Therefore in return Bali Maharaja was given a planet Sutala which is actually higher than heavenly planets, it was like a Spiritual planet and there the Lord himself appeared as his doorman to protect his abode. Because Bali maharaja surrendered everything to Vishnu, Vishnu became his servant accepting the menial position of his doorman.

Krishna becomes subservient to the surrendered devotee

Although Krishna is the proprietor, the enjoyer and the master of everything, for one who surrenders to Krishna, Krishna becomes subordinate to that love. One who is willing to be the slave of God utterly, unconditionally, only such a person does the Lord become the servant of that love. Spiritual life is slavery. “Everything for you. Nothing for me. And I have faith that you are good and your love is perfect. Therefore I have nothing to fear and surrendering everything." And as long as you are considering things by our material calculations that I have give this much to Krishna then I will only have this much left. What kind of nonsense is that? It is already Krishna’s. What is this? Who are you to give or take? Like me going into the State Bank of India and saying, “Well, I think I’ll give you a loan for one thousand dollars out of this vault, here. Does that make sense? It’s crazy. It makes better sense than what we are doing here in this world! We are saying “Krishna, I’ll donate this much to you or I’ll loan this much to you. I am very kind. It’s his! Everything is his! It is the truth." Krishna says,

sarva-dharmān parityajya
mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo
mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[Bg 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

This is the word of God. If we surrender, we should not fear. But how much we are fearful! We are afraid, if I surrender, what will my parents think. Oh, my God, they might think that I am not normal and what will my friends think, they will think 'My God, he is gone crazy' and what will my business associates think? They will think, 'Oh my God, who is this crazy fellow becoming a Hare Krishna, surrendering to Krishna!' The whole society will think that way. Madhavendra Puri, he made a prayer like this. He said, “My dear Lord Shyamsundar! He said, since I am surrendering to you, my family has rejected me as being a crazy person; the society, my friends, they all just rid me off as something that’s just beyond hope. The whole world is against me now my Lord, but I do not care. Because I know if you are pleased, that is only truth in life. Let them say what they want." So, yes. If you are surrendering to Krishna there is a great risk materially. Many people will think that this person, he is just so insecure, he is taking some crutch in the name of religion because he cannot make it in the world and be successful like me. That’s the modern day interpretation of someone who becomes religious. If you are actually really a strong successful man, you do not need God. Then if you do, you just perform some ritual and you go some once a week that’s all is required! That’s all the society asks for. Who cares what God asks for? That’s the society asks for. And the God, he knows, 'I am great, he won’t interfere too much.' And therefore, when someone takes to religion such a person thinks, 'Ah, it’s because he is weak. It’s because he cannot make it in the world. He has to take shelter of some God."So if you are surrendered to Krishna, to the degree you surrender, to that degree such materialistic persons are going to condemn you in such a way. What to speak, if you give money, people will think you are crazy; you are giving your money. What do you have left? Surrender means everything, your name, your fame.

The Super excellence of Gopis’ Surrender to Krishna

The Gopis of Vrindavan they gave up their homes, their families, their fame, even their distinction as being a religious women and even their chastity because Krishna called them. They understood that everything belongs to Krishna. Therefore the Gopis personified the ultimate renunciation,”Krishna, whatever you desire. Let your will be done." They had faith. Rupa Goswami explains that this is the basis of a devotees thinking, “Whatever I give to you Krishna, I’ll not be the looser but I’ll gain hundreds and millions and thousands of times more. Because one drop of your mercy is more valuable than everything in this creation, multiplied by millions and millions of times. Because everything in this creation will vanish today or tomorrow but one drop of your mercy is like eternal nectar that will give me life that will give me bliss that will give me inner treasures of wealth forever."So Bali Maharaja, he surrendered everything willingly with that faith that Krishna is good. And he was a demon! But because even though that he was born in a family of Asuras, because he surrendered to the Lord, he became one of the twelve Mahajanas, one of the twelve greatest authorities of spiritual life. So, it does not you know what his associates were thinking? Can you imagine? Bali maharaja - his own guru rejected him and all of the demons who were around him, the ministers, the associates they were all terribly horrified by his decision! “You are going to surrender?” He was in the phase of tremendous social pressure. He was in the phase of losing everything he worked so hard for in his life materially. But he had faith. And in the end he became so great that Vamanadev, Vishnu himself became his servant. The Lord is captured by the love of his devotee.

Krishna is eager to receive us in Goloka, our original home

When we go back to the Goloka Vrindavan the Lord greets us with warm embrace and he begins to with tears of love in his eyes, he begins to extol his appreciation,”That in order for you to come here from material world you had to endure insults, you had to give up what was most precious to you, you had to undergo many many trials, tribulations, tests, and hardships and you underwent them all for my sake. Do not think that it was unnoticed. From within your heart I was watching every moment and you have conquered me by your love, by your sacrifice, by your surrender. Now I have brought you back to your natural position of Goloka Vrindavana. Now we will enjoy our Divine Lila together eternally." That is the price of Krishna Consciousness. The price of Krishna Consciousness, pure devotional service is complete faith. That Krishna is the supreme proprietor of everything. That everything is meant for his enjoyment and that he is my best well-wishing friend in all circumstances of life.

bhoktāraṁ yajṣa-tapasāṁ
sarva-loka-maheśvaram
suhṛdaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ
jṣātvā māṁ śāntim ṛcchati

[Bg 5.29]

[A person in full consciousness of Me, knowing Me to be the ultimate beneficiary of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme Lord of all planets and demigods, and the benefactor and well-wisher of all living entities, attains peace from the pangs of material miseries.]

Surrendering to Guru, the representative of Krishna

And as my best well wishing friend whatever he does is perfect. If I simply surrender to him at every stage of life I have nothing to fear. He will protect, he will maintain, he will uplift me. And of course Krishna explains in Gita,

tad viddhi pranipatena
paripraśnena sevayā
upadekṣyanti te jṣānaṁ
jṣāninas tattva-darśinaḥ

[Bg 4.34]

[Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self-realized souls can impart knowledge unto you because they have seen the truth.]

That we surrender to Krishna in this earth by surrendering to his divine representative, the spiritual master and by surrendering to those great Sadhus, who are acting exclusively on his behalf and all the scriptures and all the faith on the Supreme benevolent merciful position of Krishna and faith in his divine representatives.

Summary learnings from Vamanadeva’s Lila

So this most beautiful story of Vamanadev is most instructive because it teaches us how even a man surrounded by demons and the Asuras, even a man who out of great aspiration for wealth and position performed tremendous tapasya to gain the whole earth and all the planets in fact, even such a person attained instant liberation, and the highest platform of the eternal pleasure in the heart of God by surrendering everything. Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, he is that same Vamana Avatar! That same Supreme Personality of Godhead! The Lord appears according to time and circumstance in different forms to fulfill his mission of establishing pure devotional service on earth. Lord Chaitanya has given us such a merciful and beautiful opportunity that what was available to Bali Maharaja is available to every living being in the human form of life in this age of Kali Yuga. If you simply surrender to the process of chanting the name of the God

[Maharaja and Devotees: Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Hare Hare]

But, of course, for our chanting, for it to have real effect, we have to have this proper service attitude. We have to chant the name of God in the spirit that I am the menial servants of the name of the Lord and the devotees of the Lord. Then the Lord will appear in his most munificent opulence within his name and by gradually becoming purified through service to the devotees and through chanting the name of God, gradually our faith develops. It becomes stronger and stronger and stronger. As our faith develops naturally our aspiration is to surrender more and more and more without fear, and with great hope and confidence in the love of God. Thank you very much. Hare Krishna!
